core_mechanism: characterizing self-similar random interface curves at a scale-invariant (critical) point by their universal fractal (Hausdorff) scaling dimensions, with the discrete set of allowed dimensions fixed by the representation/operator content of the underlying conformal symmetry algebra
brief_reason: The abstract numerically computes fractal dimensions of boundary and bulk interfaces at a critical (self-dual) point and argues the three distinct fractal-dimension values map onto the classification of primary operators of the parafermionic (conformal) algebra.
